summary: Firecrawl SDK impersonation routing scrape calls and API keys to fastcrw.com plus unverified mutable binary execution
details: Package advertises itself as a 'Python SDK for CRW web scraper' with the keyword 'firecrawl' and replicates the Firecrawl Python SDK API surface exactly (CrwClient.scrape/crawl/map/search), but its default cloud endpoint in src/crw/client.py is https://fastcrw.com/api rather than firecrawl.com. Developers reaching for Firecrawl who install this package will silently relay scrape targets and API keys to author-controlled infrastructure. In addition, src/crw/_binary.py queries https://api.github.com/repos/us/crw/releases/latest (a mutable tag on a generic 'us/crw' repo), downloads the asset with no hash or signature verification, chmods it executable, and __main__.py exec's it via os.execvp on first CLI invocation. Whoever controls the us/crw repository can ship arbitrary native code to every user of the SDK at any time. Publisher metadata (Homepage/Repository = github.com/us/crw, with the one-letter owner 'us') reinforces the impersonation pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
